text_blocks.text: Around 1300, centuries of prosperity and growth
in Europe came to a halt. A series of famines and
plagues, including the Great Famine of 1315-1377 and the
Black Death, reduced the population to around half of
what it was before the calamities. Along with
depopulation came social unrest and endemic warfare.
France and England experienced serious peasant
uprisings, such as the Jacquerie and the Peasants' Revolt,
as well as over a century of intermittent conflict in the
Hundred Years' War. To add to the many problems of
the period, the unity of the Catholic Church was shattered
by the Western Schism. Collectively these events are
sometimes called the Crisis of the Late Middle Ages.
